_note: Curated Arabic learning YouTube channels for FushaLab. Arabic type: MSA = Modern Standard Arabic / Fusha only. Playlist IDs marked UNKNOWN need manual verification in browser. Human-readable guide: docs/YOUTUBE.md

channels.name: Learn Arabic with Khasu
channels.description: One of the best MSA-only channels on YouTube. Creator from Chechnya, studied in Jordan, holds a degree in Sharia and is pursuing a Master's in Teaching Arabic to Non-Native Speakers. Exclusively Fusha — no dialects. Clear explanations, practical, modern approach.

playlists.title: Comprehensible Input for Arabic Learners
playlists.description: Slow, clear MSA speech on everyday topics — designed for learners to follow without subtitles.

playlists.title: Arabic Conversation for Beginners
playlists.description: Basic Arabic phrases and conversations. Top video has 8.3M views.

notableVideos.title: Arabic Conversation for Beginners | 70 Basic Arabic Phrases

channels.todo: Owner should find playlist IDs by visiting the channel in browser and copying from URL

channels.name: Hfz. Adnan Mrkonjic — Nauči Arapski Jezik
channels.description: The only substantial structured Arabic course in the Bosnian language on YouTube. Instructor Hafiz Adnan Mrkonjic studied Arabic in Kuwait and taught in the Arab world for 5 years. Grammar-first approach with Quranic examples. 3 levels: Level 1 (78 lessons), Level 2 (65 lessons), Level 3 (63 lessons).

playlists.title: Kurs Arapskog Jezika — 1. nivo
playlists.description: Level 1 of the structured Bosnian-language Arabic course. Covers alphabet, basic grammar, pronunciation, fundamental vocabulary.

playlists.title: Kurs Arapskog Jezika — 2. nivo
playlists.description: Level 2 — intermediate grammar, expanded vocabulary, more complex sentences.

playlists.title: Kurs Arapskog Jezika — 3. nivo
playlists.description: Level 3 — advanced grammar, reading texts, Quranic Arabic introduction.

playlists.title: Minber Academy — Arapski Jezik I Stepen
playlists.description: Separately recorded course through Minber Academy (started October 2017). Same instructor, updated format.

channels.todo: Find YouTube channel ID and playlist IDs for levels 2 and 3 by visiting nauciarapski.com or searching YouTube directly

channels.name: Imran Alawiye — Gateway to Arabic
channels.description: Author of the Gateway to Arabic textbook series. Most grammar-complete free MSA channel. Classroom-style delivery, very structured. Best for learners who want a thorough grammar foundation alongside the app's reading content.

playlists.title: Gateway to Arabic — Structured Units
playlists.description: Textbook-aligned video lessons organized by unit. Covers all foundational MSA grammar.

channels.todo: Find specific playlist IDs from channel page

channels.name: Arabic Khatawaat
channels.description: Instructor Ustaatha Raja. Step-by-step MSA/Fusha: script, alphabet, sounds, grammar, vocabulary, conversation. Three clear levels. Very good for systematic beginners.

playlists.title: Arabic Khatawaat — Level 1
playlists.description: Alphabet, script, basic vocabulary and phrases.

channels.todo: Find playlist IDs from channel page

channels.name: Madinah Arabic
channels.description: Large library of MSA content. Slow-speed explanations, dual Arabic + romanized subtitles. Good secondary resource for beginners and intermediate learners. Less visually polished but deep in content.

playlists.title: Arabic Conversation Course for Beginners
playlists.description: ~70 videos covering conversational MSA from zero.

playlists.title: Learn Arabic by Movie Clips
playlists.description: Advanced comprehensible input through Arabic film/TV clips.

channels.todo: Find playlist IDs from channel page

channels.name: Learning Arabic with Angela
channels.description: Dedicated Fusha-only channel. Practical, topic-based approach with real-life scenarios and short stories. Notable playlist: 'Learn Arabic through Short Stories' — good companion to FushaLab reading texts.

playlists.title: Learn Arabic through Short Stories
playlists.description: Short MSA stories with explanations. Directly complements FushaLab literature and culture reading texts.

channels.todo: Find YouTube channel URL and playlist IDs

channels.name: The Arabic Student
channels.description: Authentic Arabic materials — TV shows, interviews, news clips with analysis in English. Excellent comprehensible input for intermediate learners who want real Arabic, not classroom Arabic. Not structured lessons but great immersion.
channels.todo: Browse channel and add specific playlists

channels.name: Al Jazeera Arabic
channels.description: The largest Arabic-language YouTube channel (20M+ subscribers). Formal journalistic MSA. Not structured for learners — use as authentic input for advanced level. Content: news, analysis, documentaries, interviews.
channels.note: No learner scaffolding. Best used as listening challenge — play a clip, try to understand, then check understanding.

playlists.title: Al Jazeera Arabic Documentaries
playlists.description: Documentaries in formal MSA on history, culture, nature. Slower pace than news, better for learners.

channels.todo: Identify the best documentary/cultural playlists (slower speech, clearer topics) vs fast news clips

channels.name: DW عربية
channels.description: Deutsche Welle's Arabic service. Dubbed documentaries and news in formal MSA. Excellent production quality. Slightly slower and clearer than Al Jazeera — a better starting point for B2 learners dipping into authentic content.
channels.todo: Browse and identify good documentary playlists

doNotUse.name: ArabicPod101
doNotUse.reason: Mixes MSA, Egyptian, and Moroccan dialect without clear labeling — confusing for Fusha learners

doNotUse.name: Easy Arabic
doNotUse.reason: Street Arabic — Egyptian, Tunisian, Palestinian — not MSA

otherResources.name: Aswaat Arabiyya — University of Texas Austin
otherResources.description: Academic listening materials at 6 levels with speed control. Excellent leveled content, not YouTube but worth linking.

otherResources.name: Al Jazeera Learning Platform
otherResources.description: 7-level structured Arabic course — separate from the YouTube channel, a full learning platform.
